facility.name: Ag Processing, Inc.
facility.facility_description: Ag Processing, Inc. is a 292 million pounds per year methyl ester (biodiesel) plant. The biodiesel process involves reacting semi-refined or refined vegetable oils with methanol in the presence of a catalyst to produce biodiesel (methyl ester) and crude glycerin. Excess methanol is recovered and recycled back into the process. Emissions from the process and controlled with a condenser and scrubber system. This plant is adjacent to the Ag Processing, Inc. extraction/refining plant (021-0060). It is a major source of HAP emissions.

facility_totals.primary_throughput_basis: Ag Processing, Inc. is a 292 million pounds per year methyl ester (biodiesel) plant.

extraction_notes: The permit lists emission units with associated control devices but does not provide specific rated capacities for most units. Some units like EP-107 and EP-108 are identified by storage tank capacity, but no throughput or capacity is specified for the main process units. The control device CD-100 is referenced for multiple emission units but no specific performance data is provided.
